data.body:
=== RESEARCH REPORT ===
=== DSO re-explore: untouched planning/quality skills (design-review/ui-designer/retro/roadmap/etc.)
=== agent a615e022b9dfeba08

I have now read every file across all 13 skills. Let me compile the complete, lossless extraction catalog.

---

# DSO Planning & Quality Skills — Exhaustive Evaluation-Dimension Catalog

Root: `~/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/skills/`. Almost all entries are NEW (not in the prior `/Users/joeoakhart/.claude/jobs/e637fb9d/tmp/dso-dimension-catalog.md`). I read all ~50 files. Every committee reviewer dimension, gate, threshold, and verdict is listed below with literal quotes and exact line numbers. Overlaps are noted but nothing is merged.

A shared convention runs through all the committee-based skills (design-review, ui-designer, roadmap, retro): the **1–5 scoring scale** lives in `skills/shared/reviewers/SCORING-SCALE.md` (referenced, not in scope here), and the universal **pass rule** is "all dimension scores must be 4, 5, or null (N/A) to pass" at `pass_threshold: 4`. Each below cites where that rule appears.

---

## SKILL 1 — `design-review/` (six-reviewer committee; pass_threshold 4)

Skill orchestration in `design-review/SKILL.md`; roster + conflict patterns in `docs/review-criteria.md`. Final score = **min of all dimension scores / 5** (`SKILL.md:62`: "### Design Score: [min of all dimension scores] / 5"; `:64`: "(1 = Critical Failure, 3 = Passable but needs work, 5 = Design System Perfection)").

Pass gate (`docs/review-criteria.md:11-12`): "The pass threshold is **4** — all dimension scores must be 4, 5, or null (N/A) for the review to pass." Caller schema hash `1a50fe899037ef49` (`:74`).

### Reviewer A — North Star Alignment Specialist (`docs/reviewers/north-star-alignment.md`); 1–5 or null

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote (4-or-5 side) | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `user_archetype_fit` | Whether design serves a documented archetype + info hierarchy matches their workflow | "Design explicitly solves a documented need for one or more defined User Archetypes in DESIGN_NOTES; the primary beneficiary archetype is identifiable… information hierarchy is prioritized for how the archetype works" | 17 | ui-designer PM `story_alignment`/`user_value`; visual `visual_hierarchy` |
| `anti_pattern_free` | Avoids documented Anti-Patterns | "Design avoids every Anti-Pattern listed in DESIGN_NOTES; if an Anti-Pattern was tempting… there is visible evidence it was considered and rejected" | 18 | ui-designer PM `anti_pattern_compliance` (direct dup) |
| `design_system_compliance` | Visual tokens + interaction patterns + terminology trace to design system | "All visual, interaction, and language decisions trace to the documented design system in DESIGN_NOTES… no magic numbers… terminology… match the project vocabulary" | 19 | ui-designer DSL `design_system_compliance` (same name); visual `fidelity_balance` |
| `scope_fit` | Right-sized for the story | "Proposed change is scoped correctly for the story — neither over-engineered beyond the story's acceptance criteria nor under-serving the epic's intent" | 20 | ui-designer PM `scope_appropriateness` (dup); roadmap `scope` reviewer |
| `future_readiness` | Today-vs-tomorrow balance / extensibility | "Design balances 'what we need today' with 'what we need tomorrow'; obvious extensibility gaps are acknowledged; API surface or component props allow anticipated additions without a breaking change" | 21 | NEW (unique) |

Scoring: integer 1-5 or null; `future_readiness` null if no epic/backlog context (`:46`); below-4 must cite DESIGN_NOTES section (`:43-44`). Finding field `design_notes_ref` (`:61`).

### Reviewer B — Usability & HCD Specialist (`docs/reviewers/usability-hcd.md`); 1–5 or null

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `user_feedback` | Status visibility + error guidance | "User always knows what is happening and what to do next. **Status visibility**: loading indicators… session timeout warnings… **Error guidance**: errors are hard to make… messages explicitly state what happened, why, and how to fix it" | 17 | ui-designer A11y `hcd_heuristics` (Nielsen #1/#5/#9); form `validation_guidance` |
| `interaction_quality` | Expected behavior + focus/simplicity | "Interactions behave as users expect… one primary action per screen… the simplest viable option was chosen" | 18 | ui-designer PM `consistency`; reuse `trope_vs_useful` |
| `accessibility` | WCAG 2.1 AA: visual/motor/AT/responsive | "Meets WCAG 2.1 AA across abilities and devices… color contrast ≥4.5:1… touch targets ≥44px… semantic HTML… functions… on desktop, mobile, and tablet" | 19 | ui-designer A11y `wcag_compliance`+`keyboard_navigation`+`screen_reader_support`+`inclusive_design` (overlapping bundle) |
| `content_clarity` | Plain language, verb-led CTAs | "Language is plain, unambiguous… CTAs are verb-led and specific… error messages use the user's language, not system language" | 20 | NEW (closest: ui-designer A11y `screen_reader_support`) |

Scoring: 1-5 or null; `accessibility` null only for non-visual/non-interactive (`:43`); below-4 cites Nielsen heuristic / WCAG criterion / HCD principle (`:38-41`). Finding field `heuristic_ref` (`:61`).

### Reviewer C — Visual Design Specialist (`docs/reviewers/visual-design.md`); 1–5 or null

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `visual_hierarchy` | Scale/type/spacing/color guiding the eye + type scale discipline | "Effective use of scale, typography, spacing, color, and imagery to clearly convey intended functionality… 1-2 typefaces with a consistent size scale… line length… 45-75 characters for body text" | 17 | ui-designer DSL `visual_hierarchy_intent` + visual-spatial `visual_hierarchy_legibility` (split pair) |
| `intentional_layout` | Gestalt + visual balance + white space | "Every element has a clear justification for its placement. **Gestalt principles applied**: proximity… similarity… closure… **Visual balance**… **White space**: used purposefully" | 18 | NEW (unique) |
| `fidelity_balance` | High-enough/low-enough fidelity; token-derived spacing | "Design is high-fidelity enough for clear execution but low-fidelity enough for flexibility; component spacing uses token-derived values rather than pixel-exact positions; responsive considerations are visible" | 19 | overlaps frontend `specification_clarity` (different angle) |

Scoring: 1-5 or null; below-4 cites a visual principle (`:35-38`). Finding field `design_principle` (`:51`).

### Reviewer D — Senior Design System Engineer / Component Reuse (`docs/reviewers/component-reuse.md`); 1–5 or null

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `library_first` | Reuse library before custom | "Design uses existing components from the project's component library (e.g., USWDS)… before creating new ones; deviations… are justified and documented" | 17 | ui-designer DSL `component_reuse` (dup) |
| `portability` | New components reusable, general props | "New components are designed to be reusable across different contexts and pages; props/parameters are general rather than hard-coded" | 18 | overlaps DSL `new_component_justification` |
| `trope_vs_useful` | Elements earn their place | "Every UI element earns its place by serving a user need; common design patterns are applied because they solve the specific problem, not just because they are familiar" | 19 | usability `interaction_quality` (focus/simplicity) |
| `removal_impact` | Implications of removals investigated | "When UI elements are removed or replaced, the implications are investigated and documented; downstream effects on user workflows, other components, and accessibility are considered" | 20 | NEW (unique) |

Scoring: 1-5 or null; `removal_impact` null if nothing removed (`:42`). Finding field `component_ref` (`:57`).

### Reviewer E — Senior Interaction Designer / Form & Input Design (`docs/reviewers/form-input-design.md`); 1–5 or null

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `minimal_input` | Only necessary fields | "Design only requires input meaningful to the user's current process; no unnecessary fields; optional vs required is clearly distinguished" | 17 | NEW (unique) |
| `validation_guidance` | Format/validation shown proactively | "Users know what validation will be applied to input fields before they submit; format requirements are shown proactively… real-time validation" | 18 | usability `user_feedback` (error guidance) |
| `review_before_submit` | Summary/edit before submit + progress protection | "users are presented with a summary they can review before final submission; edits… are supported… user progress is preserved if the user navigates away, refreshes, or encounters an interruption" | 19 | NEW (unique) |

Scoring: 1-5 or null; `review_before_submit` null for single-field/single-action forms (`:40-41`). Finding field `form_element` (`:55`).

### Reviewer F — Technical Compliance Engineer (`docs/reviewers/tech-compliance.md`); 1–5 or null

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `stack_correct` | Libraries/frameworks from defined tech stack | "All libraries, frameworks, and tooling used in the design are from the Tech Stack defined in DESIGN_NOTES; any deviation is explicitly justified" | 17 | ui-designer frontend `implementation_feasibility` (related) |
| `architecture_consistent` | Follows system architecture patterns | "Components, data flows, and integration points follow the System Architecture patterns documented in DESIGN_NOTES; naming conventions, layer responsibilities, and module boundaries are respected" | 18 | retro Architecture `layering`/`separation` (different scope) |

Scoring: 1-5 or null; null only if no tech/arch decision relevant (`:42`). Finding field `design_notes_ref` (`:55`).

**Design-review conflict patterns** (`docs/review-criteria.md:46-51`): `expand_vs_reduce`, `add_vs_remove`, `more_vs_less`, `strict_vs_flexible` (these are conflict-resolution labels, not scored dims).

---

## SKILL 2 — `design-wireframe/`

**NO files, NO evaluation dimensions.** The directory contains only a `.DS_Store` (per the find at the start). It is effectively empty / has no SKILL.md or criteria. Flag: **no evaluation content** (likely a stub or removed skill).

---

## SKILL 3 — `ui-designer/` (4- or 5-reviewer committee; pass_threshold 4)

No top-level `SKILL.md` (confirmed: only `docs/` + `templates/`). Roster in `docs/review-criteria.md`; pass rule `:41` "ALL dimension scores must be 4, 5, or null (N/A) for the design to **pass**"; `:43` "Maximum 3 automated revision cycles. After 3 failures, escalate to the user." Caller hash `2c3ece1bc2820109` (`:88`). Conflict patterns `:52-57` (same four labels). Arbitration verdict vocabulary is `pass | needs-revision | fail` (`docs/arbitration.md`).

### Reviewer 1 — Senior Product Manager (`docs/reviewers/product-manager.md`); 6 dims, 1–5/null

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `story_alignment` | Addresses every AC | "Design directly addresses every acceptance criterion in the story" | 15 | design-review `user_archetype_fit`; roadmap value |
| `user_value` | Success State achievable, friction removed | "The Success State is clearly achievable; the design removes friction" | 16 | roadmap `value` reviewer |
| `scope_appropriateness` | Right-sized | "Right-sized: no gold-plating, no missing essentials" | 17 | **dup of design-review `scope_fit`** |
| `consistency` | Same-product feel | "Feels like part of the same product; no jarring pattern breaks" | 18 | design-review `interaction_quality`; DSL `design_system_compliance` |
| `epic_coherence` | Integrates with sibling designs / advances epic | "Design integrates naturally with sibling story designs; advances the epic's unified vision… Score null if story has no parent epic." | 19 | DSL `cross_story_consistency` (sibling angle) |
| `anti_pattern_compliance` | No Anti-Pattern violations | "Does not violate any Anti-Patterns listed in `DESIGN.md`" | 20 | **dup of design-review `anti_pattern_free`** |

Perspective `"Product Management"`; null rule for `epic_coherence` (`:39`).

### Reviewer 2 — Senior Design Systems Lead (`docs/reviewers/design-systems-lead.md`); 5 dims

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `component_reuse` | Max existing, new only if needed | "Maximizes existing components; NEW components genuinely needed" | 15 | **dup of design-review `library_first`** |
| `visual_hierarchy_intent` | Designer's *intent* (text-mediated) | "Information hierarchy intent is clear and intentional; design rationale guides the eye as designed" | 16 | paired-split with visual-spatial `visual_hierarchy_legibility`; design-review `visual_hierarchy` |
| `design_system_compliance` | Tokens/patterns/spacing | "Uses established tokens, patterns, and spacing consistently" | 17 | **dup of design-review `design_system_compliance`** |
| `new_component_justification` | New components well-specified | "New components are well-specified with clear API, justified need" | 18 | design-review `portability` |
| `cross_story_consistency` | Reuse sibling components | "Reuses components introduced in sibling story designs… Score null if story has no parent epic or no sibling designs exist." | 19 | PM `epic_coherence` |

Split note (`:21-25`): legacy `visual_hierarchy` was split (task `498b-740a-3364-4a44`); DSL now emits `visual_hierarchy_intent`, legibility owned by visual-spatial-evaluator. Perspective `"Design Systems"`.

### Reviewer 3 — CPWA Accessibility Specialist (`docs/reviewers/accessibility-specialist.md`); 5 dims

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `wcag_compliance` | All success criteria, contrast | "All applicable success criteria addressed; no known violations" | 15 | design-review `accessibility` (subsumes this) |
| `keyboard_navigation` | Keyboard completable, tab order, focus | "All interactions completable via keyboard; logical tab order; visible focus" | 16 | design-review `accessibility` |
| `screen_reader_support` | ARIA roles/labels/live regions | "Correct ARIA roles, labels, live regions; meaningful announcements" | 17 | design-review `accessibility` |
| `inclusive_design` | Reduced motion, contrast, cognitive/motor | "Accounts for reduced motion, high contrast, cognitive load, motor impairment" | 18 | design-review `accessibility` |
| `hcd_heuristics` | Nielsen #1/#5/#9 + progressive disclosure | "User always knows system status (Nielsen #1); hard to make errors (Nielsen #5); clear recovery from errors (Nielsen #9); minimal cognitive load via progressive disclosure" | 19 | **overlaps design-review `user_feedback`** |

Contrast verification rules `:43-48` (normal text 4.5:1, large 3:1, UI/graphics 3:1; missing hex → flag WCAG 1.4.3). Finding field `wcag_criterion`. Perspective `"Accessibility"`.

### Reviewer 4 — Senior Frontend Software Engineer (`docs/reviewers/frontend-engineer.md`); 4 dims

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `implementation_feasibility` | Buildable with current stack | "Buildable with current stack; no heroic workarounds needed" | 15 | design-review `stack_correct` |
| `performance` | No jank, re-renders, heavy assets | "No layout thrashing, excessive re-renders, heavy assets, or forced synchronous layouts" | 16 | NEW (unique) |
| `state_complexity` | Straightforward state, clear data flow | "State management is straightforward and maintainable; clear data flow" | 17 | retro Architecture `separation` (loosely) |
| `specification_clarity` | Implementable without questions | "A developer can implement from these artifacts without asking clarifying questions" | 18 | design-review `fidelity_balance`; roadmap `agent-clarity` |

Finding field `complexity_estimate` (`low`/`medium`/`high`) (`:52`). Perspective `"Frontend Engineering"`.

### Reviewer 5 — Visual-Spatial Evaluator (`docs/reviewers/visual-spatial-evaluator.md`); 1 dim, **pixel-observable**

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `visual_hierarchy_legibility` | Pixel-observable contrast/weight/size in rendered DOM | "Font-weight contrast, color contrast, and size differential are pixel-observable; hierarchy is legible in rendered output" | 13 | **paired-split with DSL `visual_hierarchy_intent`**; design-review `visual_hierarchy` |

Emits `verdict: pass | fail | needs-revision`, `scores`, findings with `severity: critical|major|minor|low`, `bbox_confidence: anchored|inferred`, `dom_xpath`, `dom_xpath_visually_consistent` (`:21-45`). Conditionally active (web project, Playwright, fresh route-map, budget not exhausted) — else 4-reviewer fallback (`:6-8, :48-49`). Perspective `"Visual-Spatial"`.

### Arbitration tie-break rules (`docs/arbitration.md`) — NOT scored dims; verdict-resolution gates

- Rule 1 (`:19-23`): "Pixel-observable conflicts → visual_hierarchy_legibility wins" — legibility prevails over intent on rendered measurements.
- Rule 2 (`:25-29`): "Intent-only conflicts → visual_hierarchy_intent wins" — DSL prevails on designer-intent disagreements.
- Rule 3 (`:31-33`): "Mixed tie-break (3-2…) the 3-2 majority wins regardless of which side the 5th reviewer is on."
- Rule 4 (`:35-37`): "2-2-abstain (4-reviewer fallback)… **tie defaults to block** (verdict: `needs-revision`)" → escalate to user.
- Combined verdict resolution (`:50-54`): any `fail` → `fail`; any `needs-revision` & no fail → `needs-revision`; all `pass` → `pass`. `visual_spatial_skipped: true` noted when 5th skipped (`:46`).

### Scorecard template (`templates/review-scorecard-template.md`) — recording structure

Per-reviewer tables with **Score {1-5 / N/A} + Justification**, **Recommendation: {APPROVE / REVISE / REJECT}** (`:22, :41, :59, :77`). Aggregate (`:88-94`): counts of scores at 4/5, at N/A, below 4; **Result: {APPROVED / REVISION REQUIRED / ESCALATE TO USER}**. Note: scorecard lists DSL dim as "Visual Hierarchy" (`:36`) and omits the 5th reviewer and `inclusive_design`/`hcd_heuristics` rows (template predates split — a documentation drift worth flagging).

### Output-format / manifest dimensions (artifact-quality gates, not 1-5 scores)

`docs/output-format-reference.md`: triad ID-linkage **verify gate** (`:421-424`) "For every element ID that appears in ANY artifact, verify it appears in ALL three… Flag any discrepancies before implementation." JSON rules (`:168-175`): every `id` unique + in SVG; interactive elements need `aria` role+label; EXISTING needs `design_system_ref`; MODIFIED needs `modification_notes`; NEW needs `justification`. Contrast ratios must include resolved hex for WCAG verification (`:327-331, :357-361`). `design-manifest-template.md`: Interaction Heuristic Evaluation matrix (Consequence/Frequency/Cognitive Load) (`:71-74`); Scope Assessment Foundation/Ideal/Decision (`:76-82`); Accessibility Assertions checklist incl. 44×44px touch targets (`:144-153`).

---

## SKILL 4 — `interface-contracts/`

**Pure checklist skill — NO 1-5 scored dimensions.** It defines a binary completion checklist (gate-style), not a scored rubric.

Completion checklist (`SKILL.md:113-120`), each a pass/fail gate:
- "[ ] ABC/Protocol with `@abstractmethod` decorators"
- "[ ] Complete type hints on all methods"
- "[ ] Docstrings document behavior + exceptions + thread safety"
- "[ ] Test file with contract verification tests"
- "[ ] Lint (type-check) passes: …"
- "[ ] Ticket notes include file path and constraints"

Decision gate for *whether* to create a contract (`:15-20`): "Multiple components need to communicate? / Work can be parallelized? / 3+ tasks blocked by same dependency?" → Yes triggers. Four requirements (`:25-30`). Common Pitfalls table (`:124-129`) is a problem/solution map, not scored. Flag: **checklist gates only, no scoring rubric.**

---

## SKILL 5 — `architect-foundation/`

**Largely orchestration; the "scored" content is anti-pattern risk tagging + a wiring gate, not 1-5 rubrics.**

`SKILL.md`:
- **Anti-pattern codes AP-1…AP-5** drive enforcement; questions are tagged by which AP they inform (`:74-84`): A1 "≥2 interchangeable implementations" → AP-2/3/4; A2 "mutable object or immutable messages" → AP-1; A3 "environment-specific config values" → AP-5. (AP definitions live in `skills/shared/prompts/anti-patterns.md` — out of scope here.)
- **Phase C recommendation template** scoring fields (`:94-102`): `AP code`, `Trigger`, `Layer (edit|test|CI)`, `Mechanism`, `Test isolation`, `Fit` — a structured rationale schema, not numeric.
- **Wiring gate (the real quality gate)** (`:159`): "Enforcement is **inert until wired**. Do not consider the scaffolding complete until this checklist is green" — checklist at `:155-158` (CI fitness-function entry; edit-time rule entries; populated config keys).

`fitness-function-templates.md`: these are concrete **executable assertions** (binary pass/fail fitness functions), one per AP:
- AP-3 (`:8-32`): "every OutputFormat has a handler" (variant-registry completeness).
- AP-5 (`:38-73`): "no raw env reads outside config module".
- AP-1 (`:81-105`): "test_state_is_frozen" (state immutability).
- AP-4 (`:109-147`): "every (format, mode) pair has a handler" (Cartesian completeness).
- AP-2 (`:151-174`): "no SDK types leak through interface" (abstract error hierarchy).
- Layer-registration cheat-sheet `:180-184` (edit/test/CI). 

Flag: **no 1-5 scored dimensions; AP-coded fitness-function gates (binary) + a "wired-or-inert" completion gate.**

---

## SKILL 6 — `roadmap/` (three-reviewer milestone committee + value/effort scoring)

`SKILL.md` + `docs/review-criteria.md`. Milestone fidelity review pass rule (`docs/review-criteria.md:39-40`): "Any individual dimension score below 4… **fails**… ALL dimension scores must be 4, 5, or null (N/A)… to **pass**." pass_threshold 4. Caller hash `f4e5f5a355e4c145` (`:86`). Conflict patterns `:52-56`. Reviewers live in `shared/docs/reviewers/` (agent-clarity.md, scope.md, value.md — **outside the 13-skill scope, files not read**; their dimension internals are not in these directories). Roster focus quotes (`docs/review-criteria.md:17-19`):

| Reviewer | Focus (literal) | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|
| Agent Clarity (Sr Technical PM) | "Self-contained spec, measurable success criteria" | 17 | ui-designer frontend `specification_clarity` |
| Scope (Sr Product Strategist) | "Right-sized deliverable, no overlap with other milestones, explicit dependency mapping" | 18 | design-review `scope_fit`; ui-designer `scope_appropriateness` |
| Value (Sr Product Manager) | "User or business impact, user validation signal" | 19 | ui-designer PM `user_value` |

### Value/Effort scoring (`SKILL.md` Phase 3) — a genuine 1-5 scored rubric (NEW)

- `Value (1-5)` (`:148`): "How much user or business impact does this deliver? (1=minimal, 5=critical)".
- `Effort (1-5)` (`:149`): "How complex or time-consuming is this to build? (1=trivial, 5=multi-sprint)".
- Applies the shared `value-effort-scorer.md` priority matrix → recommended P0–P4 (`:147, :151`). (Scorer file in `skills/shared/prompts/` — not read.)
- **Quadrant placement gate** (`:162-189`): Quick Wins (High Impact, Low Effort) → P0–P1; Strategic Bets (High/High) → P1–P2; Fill-ins (Low/Low) → P3; Avoid/Later (Low Impact, High Effort) → P4.
- **Critical Enabler override** (`:153-160`): "If a low-value Epic blocks a high-value Epic, it is marked as a **'Critical Enabler'** and inherits the priority of the feature it unlocks" — "Enabler Logic **overrides** the scorer recommendation."
- **Agent Alignment Test** (fidelity gate, `:221-226`, `:304-306`): "Could a developer agent understand this with no additional context? Are Success Criteria specific and testable? Are dependencies clearly documented?" Standard: "If you wouldn't trust a junior developer to build the right thing from the description alone, add more detail."
- **Ticket health gate** (`:269-275`): runs `validate-issues.sh`; "If score < 5, fix issues before finalizing." (Health score 1-5 — overlaps the `validate` repo-health score noted in CLAUDE.md.)
- **Pre-mortem** (Phase 4, `:197-211`) is qualitative risk surfacing, not scored.

---

## SKILL 7 — `prioritize-epics/` (P0–P4 assignment pipeline)

Orchestration + sub-agent prompts. The "dimensions" here are **bucket-classification criteria + a priority mapping rubric**, plus three sub-agent evaluation prompts.

### Coarse-sort bucket criteria (`prompts/coarse-sort-batch.md`) — classification rubric (NEW)

Four buckets (`:22`): "**North Star**, **optional additions**, **out of scope**, and **needs review**." Decision logic (`:33-37`): high-confidence North-Star/Out-of-Scope match vs `needs_review` (ambiguous) vs `optional_additions` (default-in-scope). **Risk flag criteria** (`:38-44`) — `risk_flag=true` if ANY of: "Touches an area the description itself calls out as fragile, legacy, or in-flight migration / Requires significant infrastructure, vendor, or external-dependency change / Cross-cutting effect on many subsystems… / Explicit 'risks' / 'open questions' / 'unknowns' section." **Avoid-risk downgrade rule** (`:46-48`): optional+risk → out_of_scope; north_star+risk → needs_review. Calibration anti-anchor (`:75`): "Do NOT use the epic's current `priority` field as a bucket signal."

### Priority mapping rubric (`phases/phase-4-granular-sort.md:7-14`) — P0–P4 derivation

| Bucket | Sub-tier | Priority | Line |
|---|---|---|---|
| Out of scope | — | P4 | 11 |
| North Star | High value | P0 | 12 |
| North Star | Low value | P1 | 13 |
| Optional additions | High value | P2 | 14 |
| Optional additions | Low value | P3 | 14 |

**High/low value scoring rubric** (`phase-4:36-37`): high value if "clearly advances the user's North Star definition, aligns with the user's stated Tradeoffs… and fits the user's Risk Tolerance"; "A North Star epic should default to high value unless one of these is significantly off." **Enabler inheritance** (`:43-46, :65-68`): "If epic A is high value, every epic that blocks A… is also high value" (fixed-point pass). **Anti-anchor rule** (`:15`): "an epic's *current* priority MUST NOT influence its new priority."

### Phase-3 dependency-adjustment rules (`phases/phase-3-coarse-sort.md:102-139`)
- Rule 1: North Star pulls blockers up from optional_additions.
- Rule 2: out-of-scope drags dependents down from optional_additions.
- Rule 3: out-of-scope blocking North Star → STOP, escalate to user (promote/drop/adjust-graph).

### Phase-2 rubric pillars (`phases/phase-2-stakeholder.md:7-12`) — the four prioritization inputs
North Star; Risk Tolerance (quick-wins vs strategic-bets vs housekeeping split); Tradeoffs (archetype/golden-path priority + functionality-vs-quality); Out of Scope. `AVOID_RISK` boolean derived (`:56-59`).

### Epic clarity-review criteria (`prompts/epic-clarity-review.md`) — gate (NEW)
"Clear enough to prioritize" = can answer all three (`:11-13`): "1. What value does it deliver? 2. Who is it for? 3. Roughly where it fits." Explicitly does NOT require spec/AC/technical approach/metrics (`:18-22`). Calibration: "Err on the side of NOT flagging" (`:69`).

### Vision-summary, vision-gap, risk-hotspots sub-agent prompts
`vision-summary.md`: extracts themes/arcs — descriptive, NOT scored (`:26-32`). `vision-gap.md`: notable_progress / notable_gaps / misalignment — "NOT a prioritization judgment" (`:60`). `risk-hotspots.md`: hotspot = "high change frequency with high failure rate" (`:6`); calibration "churn + failure together" (`:61-63`) — evidence, not scored.

---

## SKILL 8 — `validate-work/` (5 sub-agents, 2 batches; PASS/WARN/FAIL per domain — NOT 1-5)

`SKILL.md` + 6 prompts. This is a **gate skill** (detect-only, never fix; `:344-357`). Each domain emits **PASS / WARN / FAIL / SKIPPED**.

### Overall status rules (`SKILL.md:320-325`)
"PASS: all domains PASS (local checks must be PASS, not just WARN)… WARN: no domain FAIL, but at least one domain is WARN… FAIL: any domain FAIL." Final report summary matrix `:294-300` (Local / CI / Issue health / Staging deploy / Staging test).

### Per-domain gate criteria

| Domain (sub-agent) | Verdict logic (literal) | Source:line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Local validation** (SA1, haiku) | Reports Format/Lint/Type/Unit-tests/Lock-file PASS/FAIL + DB RUNNING/STOPPED/UNKNOWN; status rules: "PASS: all configured checks pass AND e2e is PASS… WARN: all static checks pass BUT e2e was SKIP or absent… FAIL: any static check fails OR e2e is FAIL/TIMEOUT" | `prompts/local-validation.md:114-118` | retro Test Quality / verification-before-completion |
| **E2E gate** (within SA1) | "`e2e: PASS` → PASS… `e2e: FAIL`/`TIMEOUT` → FAIL… `e2e: SKIP (CI passing for main)` → … report E2E: WARN" + port-conflict pre-check | `local-validation.md:54-118` | NEW |
| **CI status** (SA2, haiku) | "CI workflow status: success/failure/pending/not_found"; integration workflow SKIPPED when not configured | `prompts/ci-status.md:44-51` | verification-before-completion `CI passing` |
| **Issue health** (SA3, haiku) | "validate-issues.sh result: PASS/FAIL… Overall tickets health: PASS/FAIL" + ready/blocked/closed counts | `prompts/tickets-health.md:57-62` | roadmap ticket-health gate; retro ticket health |
| **Staging deploy** (SA4, sonnet) | exit 0=healthy / 1=unhealthy / 2=deploying (poll ≤10×30s); HTTP 200=PASS, 5xx=FAIL, unreachable=FAIL; "Overall result: PASS / FAIL / NOT_READY" | `prompts/staging-deployment-check.md:28-37, :84-108` | NEW |
| **Staging test** (SA5, sonnet) | Tiered (Tier 0 health, Tier 1 API routes, Tier 2 browser); "Overall staging test: PASS/WARN/FAIL — WARN if TEST_MODE=API_ONLY… WARN if console errors… FAIL if any Tier 0 health check failed or any Tier 2 phase FAIL" | `prompts/staging-environment-test.md:303-308` | NEW |

Gating: Batch 2 (staging test) gated on Batch 1 staging-deploy health (`:203-211`). Staging relevance classifier exit codes 0/1/2 (`:124-128`). Visual regression pre-check states pass/fail/skipped_macos/no_baselines/skipped (`:212-234`).

### `prompts/playwright-verification.md` (staging bug-fix verification)
Per-bug verdict (`:160-168`): "STATUS: RESOLVED | STILL_PRESENT | INCONCLUSIVE", TIER_USED 1/2/3, EVIDENCE; "Overall staging health: HEALTHY | DEGRADED | DOWN" (`:174`). 3-tier escalation rule (`:179`). (This prompt is referenced/shared; not wired into the SKILL.md batch but lives in the dir.)

## SKILL 9 — `verification-before-completion/`

**Behavioral gate skill — NO scored dimensions; a binary evidence gate.** `SKILL.md`.

**The Gate Function** (`:13-23`): "BEFORE claiming any status… 1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim? 2. RUN… 3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures 4. VERIFY… 5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim." **Verification command table** (`:27-35`) maps each claim → Requires vs Not-Sufficient (e.g., "Tests pass | `validate.sh --ci`… | Previous run, 'should pass'"; "Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed"; "Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports 'success'"). **Red flags** (`:37-44`): "should/probably/seems to", satisfaction before verification, committing without verification. Flag: **gate, not rubric. Overlaps validate-work and the rebar completion-verification gate conceptually.**

## SKILL 11 — `remediate-arch-evidence/` (six audit probes — gate/disposition, not 1-5 scored)

`SKILL.md`. Operates on epics tagged `arch-evidence:remediation-needed`. The "dimensions" are **6 architecture-vs-evidence probes**, each with a trigger and a NO_FLAG/FLAG verdict from a verifier sub-agent (`Phase 4:393-395`). Probe priority order 1→5→3→4→6→2 (`:216`).

| Probe | Name | What it evaluates (literal trigger) | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Probe 1 | Architectural-class self-use | "When the epic ships orchestration changes… add a self-use SC requiring the epic's own sprint to USE the deliverable on real… artifacts before sprint close" | 218-224 | NEW (dogfooding gate); loosely verification-before-completion |
| Probe 2 | Workflow-trigger audit | "When the epic introduces a new git ref pattern or modifies CI workflow triggers: enumerate trigger filters for every workflow file…" | 232-236 | NEW |
| Probe 3 | Shared-state-variable lifecycle | "When the epic introduces shared state… add a State Lifecycle Owner Block enumerating CREATE / UPDATE / CONSUME / RETIRE per variable" | 250-256 | NEW |
| Probe 4 | Bypass governance pairing | "When the epic introduces a bypass mechanism… pair with audit logging, required justification, and Phase I abuse-detection" | 280-289 | NEW |
| Probe 5 | Spec-phase coverage | "Zero-children rule: if CHILD_COUNT == 0, skip… When CHILD_COUNT > 0… apply the level-tagging pattern" (phase tags per child) | 294-301 | NEW |
| Probe 6 | External-outcome SC capture | "An in-session CLI verification table for every touchpoint… Override carve-out for genuinely-external residuals" | 320-327 | NEW |

**In-session-verifiability rule** (the master gate, `:33-52`): "Every remediation… MUST have an in-session verification command." Defines in-session-verifiable vs not. **Disposition verdicts** (`:435-437`): `:remediation-complete` (all probes verified NO_FLAG or rule-deferred) vs `:remediation-partial` (probe deferred without documented rule). Verification method: per-probe sub-agent re-check (sonnet default; opus on re-eval) (`Phase 4:387-388`). Flag: **gate/probe model, no numeric scoring.**

## SKILL 12 — `oscillation-check/` (binary detector — NO 1-5 scoring)

`SKILL.md`. **Single binary verdict: `CLEAR | OSCILLATION`** (`:69`).

Detection criteria (`:31-58`): review/critic context — iteration 1 → CLEAR; iteration 2+ diff the prior change; "If the feedback would **undo** previous changes (revert direction) → `OSCILLATION`… If… **build on** previous changes → `CLEAR`." remediation context — overlap of `files_targeted` with closed remediation tasks' files → OSCILLATION. **Safety bounds table** (`:82-88`) = max-iteration gates per caller: `/dso:review` autonomous loop `review.max_cycles` (default 4) → escalate; review total +3 buffer; debug-everything critic 2 revert cycles; sprint remediation 2 loops. Read-only (`:90-92`). Flag: **binary gate, not a rubric. Overlaps no other skill's dims (unique anti-loop guard).**

## SKILL 13 — `retro/` (five-reviewer codebase-health committee; pass_threshold 4)

`SKILL.md` + `docs/review-criteria.md` + 5 reviewers. Pass rule (`docs/review-criteria.md:13-14, :45`): "pass_threshold: 4 — all dimension scores must be >= 4 or null (N/A)". Caller hash `8a1a3dd74e54f101` (`:92`). Findings grouped Critical (P0-P1) / Improvement (P2) / Cleanup (P3-P4) (`:47`). Conflict patterns `:56-61`. Findings do NOT trigger auto code revision — become remediation tickets (`:70-76`).

### Reviewer A — Test Quality Analyst (`docs/reviewers/test-quality.md`); 5 dims

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote (4-or-5) | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `assertion_coverage` | Meaningful assertions, density ≥1.0 | "Every test function contains at least one meaningful assertion; assertion density >= 1.0; no test passes vacuously" | 16 | NEW |
| `mock_discipline` | Mocks only for external deps, <10/file | "Mocks are used only to isolate external dependencies… fewer than 10 mocks per test file…" | 17 | NEW |
| `naming_clarity` | `test_{unit}_when_{cond}_then_{outcome}` | "Test names follow the pattern… reading the name alone reveals what broke and why" | 18 | retro Naming `consistency` (different target) |
| `determinism` | Same result every run, no time/order/state deps | "All tests produce the same result on every run regardless of execution order, time of day, or environment…" | 19 | NEW |
| `risk_coverage` | High-risk paths get deeper coverage | "High-risk code paths have proportionally deeper test coverage: business logic… security boundaries… error handling… data integrity" | 20 | NEW |

Null rules `:43-50` (assertion_coverage null if no density data — bug `19ce-2d18-5d2f-4b81`; determinism null if no flaky data; risk_coverage null if no risk classification). Finding field `offending_files`. Perspective `"Test Quality"`.

### Reviewer B — Documentation Health Specialist (`docs/reviewers/documentation.md`); 3 dims

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `freshness` | No stale issues/refs/REVIEW-DEFENSE | "KNOWN-ISSUES.md has no unarchived resolved issues; README and CLAUDE.md contain no deprecated API references… `# REVIEW-DEFENSE:` comments reference patterns that still exist" | 16 | NEW |
| `completeness` | TODO/FIXME tracked (<20 or ticketed) | "TODO/FIXME count is below 20 or all items are tracked as ticket tasks; no FIXME comments older than 60 days without a corresponding tracking issue" | 17 | code-quality `dead_code` (comment noise) |
| `navigability` | TOC/cross-refs/consistent structure | "Documents longer than 100 lines have a table of contents or clear heading hierarchy; related docs cross-reference each other… consistent structure across similar doc types" | 18 | NEW |

Null rules `:44-46`. Finding field `stale_location` (for freshness). Perspective `"Documentation"`.

### Reviewer C — Senior Software Engineer / Code Quality (`docs/reviewers/code-quality.md`); 4 dims

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `file_size` | No files >500 lines (unjustified) | "No source files exceed 500 lines; files that exceed 500 lines have a documented justification" | 16 | rebar CLAUDE.md module-size policy (cap 800) |
| `complexity` | Functions ≤50 lines, nesting ≤4 | "No functions or methods exceed 50 lines; no nesting deeper than 4 levels; complex logic is decomposed…" | 17 | ui-designer frontend `state_complexity` (loosely) |
| `duplication` | No 3+ repeats of 10+ line blocks | "No significant repeated patterns (3+ occurrences of the same 10+ line block); DRY violations are tracked as ticket tasks if intentionally deferred" | 18 | NEW |
| `dead_code` | No unreachable/unused/orphaned code | "No unreachable functions, unused imports, orphaned modules, or abandoned feature flags…" | 19 | documentation `completeness` (commented-out code) |

Null rules `:44-46` (duplication null if no scan; dead_code null if no static analysis). Finding field `violating_location`. Perspective `"Code Quality"`.

### Reviewer D — Style and Conventions Auditor / Naming (`docs/reviewers/naming-conventions.md`); 1 dim

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `consistency` | Identifier casing per dominant convention | "All identifiers follow the conventions established by the project's language and framework — each category… uses a single consistent style with no mixed casing… Evaluate against the dominant convention per category" | 17 | test-quality `naming_clarity` (test-name angle) |

Multi-language defaults table inline (`:17`). Null only if no naming check run (`:45`). Finding field `convention_violated`. Perspective `"Naming Conventions"`.

| Dim | Evaluates | Literal quote | Line | Overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|

| `separation` | No circular imports, SharedState discipline | "No circular import chains; interfaces used appropriately at layer boundaries; `SharedState` is the sole shared state… configuration accessed through `BaseConfig` not `os.environ`" | 18 | architect-foundation AP-5 config-boundary; AP-1 state |
| `error_resilience` | Explicit error handling/retry/boundaries | "External I/O calls… have explicit error handling with retry policies or graceful degradation; no bare `except:`… error boundaries exist at layer transitions" | 19 | usability `user_feedback` (error messages, loosely) |
| `observability` | Structured logging, timing, correlation IDs | "Structured logging at service boundaries… pipeline stage durations are measurable… correlation IDs or job IDs propagate… error logs include enough context… without logging sensitive data" | 20 | NEW |

Null rules `:50-52` (separation null if no circular-import detection; observability null if no logging audit). Finding field `invariant_violated`. Perspective `"Architecture"`.

### Retro non-committee scored/gate content (`SKILL.md`)
- **Friction-suggestion thresholds** (`:57-60`): "Clusters with `count >= 3` are high-signal and should become P2… `count < 3`… P3."
- **Shift-left CI gate table** (`:82-92`): each failure category → earliest gate + gap → finding `{category, gate, gap, recommendation}`.
- **Findings tiering** (`:126-132`): Critical (P0-P1) / Improvement (P2) / Cleanup (P3-P4) with shift-left placement; **fix-vs-build tiebreaker** "A task to 'complete the routing table' is always preferred over 'build an agent metadata system'."
- **Bug classification thresholds** (`:140-162`): recurrence_threshold (default 3) fires labeled findings; classifier-failed threshold=1.
- **Inference-incident corpus gate** (Phase F, `:212-309`): cadence 90 days; `CORPUS_INSUFFICIENT` if <20 validated incidents; 5 required schema fields.

| Skill | Has 1-5 scored dims? | What it has instead |
|---|---|---|
| design-review | **Yes** (6 reviewers, 21 dims) | + min-score gate, conflict patterns |
| design-wireframe | **No — empty** | only `.DS_Store`; no SKILL.md/criteria |
| ui-designer | **Yes** (5 reviewers, 21 dims) | + arbitration tie-break rules, verdict resolution, scorecard |
| interface-contracts | **No** | binary completion checklist + decision gate |
| architect-foundation | **No** | AP-coded fitness-function gates (binary) + wiring gate; structured recommendation schema |
| roadmap | **Yes** (3 milestone reviewers + Value/Effort 1-5 + health-score<5 gate + Agent Alignment Test) | quadrant→priority mapping, enabler override |
| prioritize-epics | **No numeric** | bucket-classification rubric + high/low-value rubric + P0–P4 mapping + clarity gate + risk-flag criteria |
| validate-work | **No** (PASS/WARN/FAIL per domain) | 5-domain gate matrix, batch gating |
| verification-before-completion | **No** | binary evidence gate (Gate Function + command table) |
| dryrun | **No — pure orchestration** | mutation-blocking only |
| remediate-arch-evidence | **No** | 6 audit probes (NO_FLAG/FLAG) + in-session-verifiability gate + complete/partial disposition |
| oscillation-check | **No** | binary CLEAR/OSCILLATION detector + max-iteration safety bounds |
| retro | **Yes** (5 reviewers, 17 dims) | + tiering, shift-left gate, thresholds, corpus gate |

**Pure-orchestration / no-evaluation flags:** `dryrun` (zero evaluation content), `design-wireframe` (empty stub). **Gate-only (no rubric):** `interface-contracts`, `verification-before-completion`, `validate-work`, `oscillation-check`, `architect-foundation`, `remediate-arch-evidence`, `prioritize-epics`.

## Cross-skill duplicate dimensions (literal name/intent collisions)
- `design_system_compliance` — design-review (north-star) **and** ui-designer (DSL): same name, same intent.
- `anti_pattern_free` (design-review) ≈ `anti_pattern_compliance` (ui-designer PM): direct dup.
- `scope_fit` (design-review) ≈ `scope_appropriateness` (ui-designer PM) ≈ `scope` (roadmap reviewer): right-sizing dup ×3.
- `library_first` (design-review reuse) ≈ `component_reuse` (ui-designer DSL): dup.
- design-review `accessibility` (one dim) = ui-designer A11y reviewer's four dims (`wcag_compliance`/`keyboard_navigation`/`screen_reader_support`/`inclusive_design`) — one rolled-up vs four split.
- design-review `user_feedback` ≈ ui-designer A11y `hcd_heuristics` (both Nielsen #1/#5 status+error).
- **Paired split (intentional, not dup):** ui-designer `visual_hierarchy_intent` (DSL, text-mediated) vs `visual_hierarchy_legibility` (visual-spatial, pixel) — both descend from design-review's single `visual_hierarchy`; arbitration rules govern conflicts.
- retro `naming_clarity` (test names) vs retro `consistency` (identifier casing): adjacent, different targets.
- `value`/`effort` 1-5 (roadmap) vs high/low-value rubric (prioritize-epics): both feed P0–P4 but via different mechanics (scorer matrix vs bucket sub-tier).

**Key file paths:** all under `/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/skills/<skill>/` as enumerated. The shared reviewer files referenced by roadmap (`shared/docs/reviewers/agent-clarity.md`, `scope.md`, `value.md`) and the shared scorer (`shared/prompts/value-effort-scorer.md`), scoring scale (`shared/reviewers/SCORING-SCALE.md`), and anti-patterns (`shared/prompts/anti-patterns.md`) live **outside** the 13 named directories and were not in scope — their internal dimension definitions are not captured here and would need a follow-up read if required.
